candidate.title: Corigine Agilio CX 2x25GbE SmartNIC structured product fields

local_checks.repo_state: Planner evidence recorded unrelated untracked .codex/generated/docs artifacts; generator reused only local ai_infra raw/wiki/data evidence and did not fetch external sources.
local_checks.raw_manifest_scan: Searched local compute accelerator raw captures, source registry, structured catalog YAML, Compute Accelerator Crawl Inventory, Compute Accelerator Parameter Comparison, Network Storage Cluster Infrastructure, coverage-map.json, loop-state.json, and r9/r10 hardware manifests for DPU, SmartNIC, IPU, storage offload, NVMe-oF, product-specific, line rate, port count, PCIe, memory, IOPS, latency, power, offload engine, schema field, duplicate boundary, aggregate, multi-variant, and unresolved-field terms.
local_checks.wiki_search: Existing curated pages already listed Corigine Agilio CX as raw DPU/SmartNIC comparison evidence, but the structured catalog had no Corigine SKU, observation, or resolved rows before this task; Resnics N1025/S1100, Biren, Cambricon, Kunlunxin, BlueField-3, and Intel E2100 resolved rows were treated as duplicate boundaries.
local_checks.domain_index_scan: The ai_infra index points to Compute Accelerator Spec Catalog, Compute Accelerator Parameter Comparison, Field Glossary, Spec Sources, AI Infra Coverage Map, and Network Storage Cluster Infrastructure; no new page was needed.
local_checks.schema_scan: spec-fields.yaml already supports form_factor, network_bandwidth for dpu/ipu, memory_type, memory_capacity, and host_interface, so no schema extension was needed for the Corigine slice.
local_checks.completed_r9_r10_boundaries: r9 resolved Cambricon MLU370-X4/X8 and Kunlunxin RG800; r10 resolved Biren deployment-envelope fields and Resnics Stargate-N1025/S1100 DPU/storage-offload fields. This task avoided those products except as duplicate boundaries.

gap_reason: The hardware layer still had a local-evidence-first SmartNIC gap: the official Corigine Agilio CX 2x25GbE product brief exposes product-specific port, memory, and PCIe host-interface values with explicit units, while the structured catalog had only raw comparison text for Corigine. Promoting this bounded slice improves DPU/SmartNIC product coverage without claiming benchmark, protocol, aggregate-system, packet-processing throughput, power, latency, or production-operations behavior.

duplicate_checks.finding: No existing corigine-agilio-cx-2x25gbe SKU row was present before this task; BlueField-3, Intel E2100, Resnics N1025/S1100, Biren, Cambricon, and Kunlunxin rows were already represented and not duplicated.
No existing resolved fields pointed to corigine-agilio-cx-product-brief before this task.
Corigine Agilio CX was already visible as a raw comparison row with 2 x 25GbE ports, 2 GB DDR3 memory, and PCIe Base 3.0 x8 wording, so this task promoted only those same source-backed fields.

The network/storage page separates product-specific DPU/SmartNIC catalog fields from SPDK protocol behavior, MLCommons benchmark claims, and incident/postmortem evidence; this task did not broaden Corigine fields into architecture or benchmark claims.
Prior Resnics task explicitly left Corigine as useful PDF comparison evidence; the continuation task uses that remaining exact product-brief boundary rather than repeating the Resnics slice.

selected_source_backed_fields.source_boundary: Official Corigine Agilio CX 2x25GbE SmartNIC PDF product brief. network_bandwidth records aggregate data-port bandwidth from the source-stated 2 x 25GbE ports; host_interface preserves PCIe Base 3.0 compatible with PCIe 1.1/2.0 and x8 link wording.

rejected_or_deferred_fields.reason: The source lists offload features and flow-table/tunnel capacities, but the current catalog schema does not model those fields and the task goal was a narrow port, memory, and host-interface slice.

rejected_or_deferred_fields.record: Corigine Agilio CX power, latency, and IOPS
rejected_or_deferred_fields.reason: The product brief text reviewed locally does not expose card power, storage IOPS, or latency fields with explicit product units, so no values were promoted.

Helium exposes useful model rows such as EC2004Y and EC2002P, but the page mixes model variants, product-line claims, and NFV comparison rows; it remains raw comparison evidence until a future task promotes explicit per-model boundaries.

rejected_or_deferred_fields.record: Asterfusion CX102S-DPU, Dayu Paratus, Nebula DPU/RNIC/SNIC, Yusur K2-Pro/SWIFT-2200N, and Resnics N2025
rejected_or_deferred_fields.reason: These rows remain duplicate-boundary or raw comparison evidence because they are aggregate gateways, broad product pages, JS-heavy/incomplete captures, mixed chip/product-series sources, or were intentionally outside this bounded Corigine slice.

rejected_or_deferred_fields.record: Protocol, benchmark, architecture, cloud, and runtime sources
rejected_or_deferred_fields.reason: SPDK, MLCommons Storage, NCCL fabric blogs, HyperPod docs, cloud offering pages, and runtime issue logs were not used as product-specific Corigine field evidence.
